notes.note: Letter-carriers from Atticus's household (Latin pueri, "boys"). Private correspondence travelled by such slaves, who often waited to take a reply back.

Publius Autronius Paetus, a convicted Catilinarian conspirator living in exile in Greece. Cicero, who had testified against him, dreads coming within four days' march of him and other personal enemies.
The mountainous stretch of the Via Egnatia between Dyrrachium and Macedonia. The overland alternative to halting in Epirus.

notes.anchor: Sent the day before the Kalends of May
notes.note: Pridie Kalendas Maias, i.e. 30 April 58 BC. The day immediately before the first of the month.
